stem: A clinical research study measures treatment response rate across 5 trial sites, each containing multiple patient cohorts. trial site A has 5 observations with mean 53.1 % (within-trial site SD = 6.4). trial site B has 10 observations with mean 47.7 % (within-trial site SD = 7.2). trial site C has 5 observations with mean 59.8 % (within-trial site SD = 7.2). trial site D has 15 observations with mean 37.2 % (within-trial site SD = 3.2). trial site E has 15 observations with mean 39.2 % (within-trial site SD = 9.4). The between-trial site standard deviation is uncertain, estimated between 6.2 and 15.6 %. Using a hierarchical Bayesian model, estimate the true treatment response rate for trial site A, accounting for shrinkage toward the grand mean and the uncertainty in the between-trial site variation.

ground_truth.answer: Hierarchical estimate for trial site A: 52.4 % (shrunk from raw 53.1). Range [51.5, 52.8] as between-trial site SD varies from 6.2 to 15.6. Pooled mean: 43.8.

solution_steps: Compute pooled (grand) mean: 43.8 % (weighted average of 5 trial site means).
Shrinkage factor for trial site A: B = σ²_between / (σ²_between + σ²_within/5). At σ_between=10.4: B = 0.93.
Interval [51.5, 52.8] captures uncertainty from the unknown between-group variance.
common_failure_modes: Using the raw observed mean (53.1) without shrinkage
Using only the grand mean (43.8) and ignoring group-specific data
Not recognizing that small-sample groups should be shrunk more toward the grand mean
Treating the between-group variance as known rather than uncertain
